summary.narrative: Bass delivered fully on several discrete executive commitments — declaring a homelessness state of emergency on day one, issuing the affordable-housing acceleration directive (ED1, later codified), and ordering identification of city-owned property for housing. Larger outcome-based pledges showed partial progress: street homelessness fell roughly 17.5% two years running but fell far short of the promised 'end' to it, and police-force growth stalled and was partly reversed amid budget constraints. Her personal pledge to limit out-of-town travel was broken by a trip to Ghana during the Palisades fire.
summary.grade_rationale: The record mixes clean, well-documented fulfillment of unilateral executive promises (high delivery, high role) with substantial but incomplete progress on the hardest structural goals (homelessness, policing), where documented action exists but goal-fractions and scope reductions cap delivery, and one broken binary personal pledge. Strong execution on H1 directives is offset by under-delivery on H2/H3 quantitative commitments.

promises.text: Declare a local emergency on homelessness on the first day in office.
what_happened: 2022-12-12: Bass declared a state of emergency on homelessness as her first act in office and activated the Emergency Operations Center.
2025-11-04: Bass lifted the emergency declaration, citing tools now in place.

promises.text: End street homelessness / end people sleeping in parks or on the street (campaign pledge associated with a 2026 timeframe).
what_happened: 2022-12-21: Bass signed early executive directives targeting encampments.
2023-onward: Inside Safe launched and resolved 100+ encampments.
2026-05-20: Bass publicly admitted falling short of ending street homelessness by 2026.
2026-06-03: Reported street homelessness down 17.5% two years in a row.

promises.text: Issue an executive directive to accelerate and lower the cost of building affordable and temporary housing in Los Angeles.
what_happened: 2022-12: Bass signed Executive Directive 1 to fast-track and cut costs of affordable housing.
2023-06/07: City Council codified ED1 by ordinance.
2023-09-14: Mayor's office announced 7,000+ accelerated affordable units.
2026-04-27: Office reported 42,000+ units in pipeline under permitting reforms.

promises.text: Identify city-owned properties to use for housing.
what_happened: 2023-02: Bass signed Executive Directive 3 seeking a 20-day report of vacant/surplus/underutilized city-owned properties.
2023-05-16: Mayor released an update on identified city-owned land for interim and permanent housing.
2024-04: Sites identified for up to 500 tiny home beds via $33M state grant.

promises.text: Increase the number of city police officers.
what_happened: 2023-08: LAPD reported largest single-month applications since 2020.
2025-06-07: Bass-signed budget reduced planned police hiring from 480 to 240.
2025-12-12: Bass urged Council to find $4.4M to continue LAPD hiring; Council approved one class of 40 recruits.
2026-05-21: Council backed budget funding 510 officers (LAPD ~8,555 by 2027).

promises.text: Promised that as mayor the only places she would travel would be D.C., Sacramento, San Francisco and New York, in relation to L.A.
what_happened: Early 2025: Bass was in Ghana for a presidential inauguration when the Pacific Palisades fire erupted; she returned to Los Angeles after the fire began.

data_gaps: Several campaign-era promises (c1, c2, c14) are Tier-3 and the exact campaign-stated homelessness deadline ('by 2026' / 'end street homelessness') is reconstructed from later reporting (c93-c95) rather than a verbatim 2022 campaign source; treated as date_uncertain. Environmental/climate pledges (c3-c8) were excluded for lack of attributable, forward-looking, verifiable campaign framing tied to a specific target despite delivery-side evidence (c111, c134, c146). The travel pledge (c96) is quoted from later commentary citing the campaign statement; corroborated by c99 but no original campaign transcript supplied. Time_pressure left null throughout because campaign-stated deadlines are not cleanly datable from the material.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 88.0% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
